title: He Holds 129 World Records. He Just Set His 130th — on Fire.
script: 6 minutes. On literal fire? David Rush of Boise, Idaho — the man who holds the most concurrent Guinness World Records in existence, 129 at last count — just added another one. On June 12th, he picked up 3 flaming torches and kept them in the air for 6 minutes and 25.49 seconds straight. A new world record: longest duration juggling flaming torches. Rush is a STEM educator who breaks records to inspire students to study math and science. He's juggled axes. Balanced bikes on his chin. Caught flaming machetes. But 6 minutes of open flame, constantly in motion — he said one missed catch and it's over. The man holds the record for the most world records ever. Could David Rush actually run out of records to break?
